Climate change poses physical, transitional, and reputational risks across the entire value chain from raw material sourcing to operations and end-of-life of products. Increasing regulatory pressure, stakeholder expectations, and investor scrutiny demand proactive climate action, transparent disclosure, and value chain collaboration to mitigate Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions.

Polyplex addresses climate change across its value chain by sourcing more low-carbon raw materials and producing rPET film with post-consumer recycle content of upto 100%. Within its operations, the company invests in biomass-based energy, energy-efficient systems, and renewable energy procurement while tracking emissions per GRI 305 and improving performance under ISO 50001. Water recycling further reduces impacts. Polyplex designs recyclable, mono-material, and low-impact films, conducts LCAs, and supports customers with recycling and EPR initiatives. Climate risks are aligned into risk management
Improper waste handling leads to environmental degradation, regulatory penalties, and brand risk. Circular economy practices are now demanded by global brands and regulators.

Polyplex practices 3Rs (Reduce, Reuse, Recycle), reprocesses in-house PET/BOPP scrap, and manufactures Sarafil rPET films from 100% recycled content. Waste segregation, disposal via authorized PCB vendors, and collaboration with recyclers are in place.
High energy intensity increases cost and carbon footprint. Regulatory mandates like ISO 50001 require improved efficiency.

Polyplex has deployed VFD-enabled blower systems, optimized pump speeds, and replaced inefficient air handling units. ISO 50001 certified EMS guides energy-saving actions across plants. Sites monitor specific energy consumption and report progress annually.
Water scarcity, uneven availability, and regulatory scrutiny make efficient water use a priority. Industrial zones face increasing pressure to reduce discharge and increase recycling.
Plants in India operate reuse treated water for landscaping and cooling towers and have replaced soft water with raw water in non-critical areas.
Operations near ecologically sensitive areas can disrupt habitats, leading to reputational and compliance risks. Biodiversity protection is increasingly embedded in global ESG frameworks (GRI 304).

Greenbelt development, afforestation, and buffer zones are created around plants. Wastewater is treated before discharge to avoid soil or water contamination. Biodiversity risks are assessed before expansion or land development projects.

Polyplex develops recyclable mono-material structures, heavy-metal free BOPET, and solvent-free printable films. Life Cycle Assessments (LCA) are conducted. Products comply with REACH, FDA, and EFSA guidelines. End-use sustainability is considered during product design.

R&D centers in India focus on rPET films, chemical recycling, and down-gauging. The team collaborates with OEMs and customers to co-develop low-impact films. Polyplex aligns with SDGs 9, 12, 13 and MSCI metrics.
Accidents or unsafe conditions lead to legal liabilities, productivity loss, and reputational damage.

Polyplex has implemented ISO 45001 systems, safety audits, near-miss reporting, and emergency preparedness drills. Training and TRIR metrics are monitored and reported. Regular internal and external audits ensure ongoing compliance.

Engaged employees drive innovation, retention, and productivity. Surveys, performance reviews, open communication channels, rewards, and recognition programs help maintain engagement. Exit feedback and internal growth opportunities are encouraged.
Regular upskilling through technical and soft-skills training, leadership development, and cross-functional learning. Scholarships and career progression programs are available to all employees.

Customer loyalty and trust depend on product quality, responsiveness, and innovation. Robust customer feedback mechanisms, continuous quality improvement programs, and supply chain reliability ensure high satisfaction levels.
Human rights policies, POSH compliance, grievance redressal, Supplier assessments, Trainings and Code of Conduct enforcement are in place
Vendor due diligence, screening against sustainability criteria (e.g., EcoVadis), conflict-free mineral declarations, and local supplier engagement are followed.

Transparent governance ensures investor confidence and regulatory compliance. Our business philosophy is based on the concepts of fairness, transparency, accountability and responsibility. Independent Board oversight, structured risk management, and regular disclosures are in line with SEBI and GRI.
The Company has implemented Anti-bribery, Code of Ethics, whistle blower policy, ethics training, and internal audits
Non-compliance can lead to business interruptions, license cancellations, or penalties.
Dedicated compliance teams track laws globally, align reporting with SEBI BRSR, GRI, and ensure alignment with environmental and labor regulations.

Polyplex recognises the importance of environmental stewardship and strives to reduce waste through the usage of the 3 R’s (Reuse, Reduce and Recycle). We minimize manufacturing waste through source reduction, reuse and recycling, and responsibly disposing of hazardous waste. Waste from direct materials, packaging materials and plant operations are the three categories of waste generated by our operations (hazardous and non-hazardous). Because PET in both film and resins is often recyclable, the polyester film manufacturing process has a minimal environmental impact. We have the required EIA approval for its Polyester resin line, and we provide periodical reports to the competent authorities as required by the Environmental Clearance (EC).

Waste from direct materials: 
Non-hazardous waste like polyester chip lumps, polyester film lumps, waste trimmings, contaminated films, and waste aluminium coated film. We recycle and reuse 90% of our simple PET film waste and the remaining is sold to
third-party contractors.

Waste from packaging materials:
Waste like wooden pallets, plastics, PVC cups, and paper cores as packaging material for both incoming material, as well as finished goods. We re-use packaging material internally multiple times and the remaining is sold to
third parties.

Waste from plant operations: 
Waste like sludge from ETP and tank bottom, oil and lubricants, scrap metal, batteries, chemical waste, and used drums. Most of the waste is non-hazardous and sold to third parties. Hazardous waste is disposed to authorized vendors.

Yes, the Company adheres to Occupational Health and Safety Management System and has a robust mechanism 
for emergency response and disaster management aligned to the global standards in the industry. Workplace 
safety is of utmost priority and the Company is committed to providing a safe and healthy work environment 
for the employees. We are consistently making efforts to enhance health and safety standards by improving 
working conditions, raising awareness through involvement, participation and continuous training of the workforce. Our approach to safety is encapsulated in our safety, health and environment (SHE) policy that validates our commitment to protecting the health and safety of all employees, contractors, customers and the communities in which it operates.

The Company uses several processes to identify work-related hazards and assess risks on a routine basis. Some 
of which are as below:

1. HIRA (Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment) – This is one of the integral aspects of the planning process. 

2. HAZOP (Hazard and Operability) study – The Company collaborates with external safety experts to conduct 
HAZOP analysis, comprehensive safety audits, thermography analysis, testing of pressure vessels and Lifting 
tools, etc.

3. Regular Safety Audits – The safety representatives conduct periodical safety audits which help to understand 
whether all standard operating procedures (SOPs) are followed, systems are in place, any deviation identified, 
and corrective actions are taken on time.

4. Health & Safety Committees – An effective safety culture requires proactive commitment, accountability, 
and continuous reinforcement from all levels of management. The culture is driven by the top management 
and executed at every level through the health and safety committee. Our health and safety committee 
oversee the overall implementation of our safety policies and reviews its performance.

5. Health and Safety training – Our safety representatives engage with employees on safety management 
processes and impart training on health, safety, security, emergency preparedness and crisis management regularly. It is provided to not just our company employees but also to contract workmen and security 
personnel, which are trained through experts and in-house safety personnel. Training conducted covered 
topics such as fire safety, electrical safety, first aid, gas cylinder/ammonia handling and awareness about 
safety signs

6. Emergency Preparedness Plan – Polyplex maintains thorough emergency preparedness plans for all sites, 
ensuring rapid response to incidents. These plans include on-the-job training, adequate fire protection facilities, 
regular mock drills, and access to critical resources such as emergency equipment and medical assistance

7. Reporting of near miss cases and regular review of monitoring unsafe conditions.

The Company is committed to the health and safety of the employees. Several initiatives taken by the Company are as below:

- Periodic Health Check-up of employees

- Regular medical camps, awareness sessions, and medical support services

- Health and Safety trainings

- Preparing for emergencies and conducting regular mock drills

- Environmental monitoring conducted as per requirements

- EHS pillar framed and monthly meetings conducted to discuss EHS related issues and closures

- Removal of unsafe conditions from work place

Safety is an important tool to augment our productivity and reduce losses. The Company encourages people to 
spend more time reflecting on and improving their understanding of the safety risks that may arise in the workplace. Several initiatives have been undertaken to sensitize employees regarding the occupational hazards at the workplace, precautions and emergency responses for countering any accident, etc.

Polyplex prioritize safeguarding and upholding human rights in all facets of our operations. We are committed to 
operating in a manner consistent with the United Nations (UN) universal declaration of human rights, the 10 UN 
Global Compact (UNGC) principles and the applicable international labor organization (ILO) core conventions on labor standards. The Company takes care that none of the transactions infringes on any human rights. The dignity and rights of employees, customers, vendors, and all other stakeholders are valued. Human rights training and the establishment of approved norms of behavior while working with all stakeholders are critical.

The Company is committed to a workplace free of discrimination and harassment, and has zero tolerance for such 
unacceptable conduct. The Company encourages reporting of such incidents and is responsive to complaints. 
Mechanisms to prevent adverse consequences to the complainant are taken care by various policies including POSH.

Yes, the Company has a mechanism for zero liquid discharge. There is no discharge of untreated water at any of these locations. The treated water from STP is used for landscaping and horticultural reasons at our India business. The treated water from ETP is mostly used for road washing purpose.

ETP and STP Plants are in operation for treatment of wastewater discharge.

The Company stresses upon measures for the conservation and optimal utilization of greenhouse-gas emissions in all the areas of operations, including those for energy generation and effective usage of sources/equipment used for generation. There are continuous efforts to improve operational efficiencies, minimize consumption of resources and reducing energy and Co2 emissions while maximizing production volume.

Besides, multiple initiatives in the past, there are various on-going energy conservation initiatives at the plant and some of them are as highlighted below:

1. Replacement of Air Handling Unit blowers with Energy efficient blowers (with VFD control) in the following areas:

• Winder Slitter
• Secondary Slitter
• MDO - 1 & 2
• Holography
• PCC room

2. Replacement of Air washer blowers with Energy efficient blowers (with VFD control) in the following areas:


• RMS Roof
• Utility Roof
• BOPP Switch board - 1A, Switch board - 1B, Switch board -2 and BOPP Lab

3. Power Factor improvement from 0.995 to 0.998 by additional Capacitor Banks- 400 KVAR phase-II

4. Increase of Husk Heater efficiency by 1%

5. Reduction of water treatment chemical by 10%

6. Phased wise revamping of Hot insulation of Husk heater to Intec HTM & Cold insulation of Chiller line.

We place great emphasis on efficient waste management practices and are committed to ensuring that all types 
of waste are treated and disposed of properly. Our approach to waste management is guided by the 3R principle of ‘Reduce, Reuse and Recycle’. Most of our industrial process waste is recycled inhouse.

Embracing a dynamic product design strategy, we place sustainability and circular economy principles at the forefront. Our focus on eco-friendly products, including recycled PET (rPET) films and mono-PET structures, exemplifies our dedication to sustainable innovation that minimizes environmental impact and enhances resource efficiency. We developed and optimized “chemical recycling” process for manufacturing Sarafil rPET.

Polyester film with post-consumer recycle content of upto 100% for packaging applications. The film has been made available commercially using postconsumer PET bottle flakes as input material. The rPET resin has properties same as that of virgin PET resin and the resultant PET film is compliant with regulatory requirements including EC and US FDA compliances.

Our waste streams, which include waste from direct materials, packaging materials, and plant operations (both 
hazardous and non-hazardous), are inventoried periodically and are sent to third party for recycling / disposal in 
compliance with applicable government regulations.

Waste Management Practices

A) Plastic waste is sold to buyers who are registered with SPCB/CPCB under Plastic Waste Management Rule 2016. 

B) E Waste generated is collected /stored & disposed as per E Waste Management Rule and disposed to authorized E waste management agency by State Pollution control board.

C) Bio Medical Waste generated is collected/stored & disposed as per Bio Waste Management Rules and disposed 
to authorized biomedical waste management agency by State Pollution control board.

D) Any Civil /Demolition waste is used in house for landfilling.

E) Battery Waste generated is collected/stored/disposed as per Battery Management Rules and disposed to 
authorized dealer of manufacturer who are registered with respective state pollution control board for recycling 
of old Batteries.

F) Radioactive waste is sent back to original supplier. They send their authorized representative to collect the same to the Company’s factory, who take radioactive waste in custody in their original radiation preventive packing. 

G) For other Hazardous waste, the Company collects/store/disposes waste as per Hazardous waste rule and disposed the same to SPCB/CPCB authorized waste management agency.
 
H) For Other waste like Metal/Paper/Wood, the Company conducted auctions on six monthly basis and same is sold to aggregators/recyclers.
Plastics Export Promotion Council of India (‘PLEXCONCIL-India’)
Indian Flexible Packaging and Folding Carton Association (‘IFCA-India’)
